title: helianthemum vineale
intro: Helianthemum vineale, also known as the rockrose or frostweed, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and woody stems. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Helianthemum vineale are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote overall health. Herbal preparations of Helianthemum vineale are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
